# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for a Network Security Group that allows our remote development team to access Azure VMs. Include rules for RDP (port 3389), SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source (0.0.0.0/0). The team works globally and needs flexible access.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud network security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_network_security

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019/04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0

parameters:
  nsgName:
    type: string
    defaultValue: nsg-remote-dev-team
    metadata:
      description: Name of the Network Security Group

  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    metadata:
      description: Location for the NSG resource

  # SECURE: Use explicit allowlists instead of 0.0.0.0/0
  # List your team's known IP ranges or VPN egress IPs
  allowedManagementSourceIPs:
    type: array
    metadata:
      description: >-
        Allowlisted IP addresses/CIDR ranges for RDP and SSH access.
        Use your corporate VPN egress IPs or team members static IPs.
        NEVER use 0.0.0.0/0 for management ports.
    # No default value - forces explicit specification at deployment time

  allowedWebSourceIPs:
    type: array
    defaultValue:
      - "0.0.0.0/0"
    metadata:
      description: >-
        Source IPs allowed for HTTP/HTTPS. 0.0.0.0/0 is acceptable
        for web traffic if the VMs serve public web applications.

resources:
  - type: Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups
    apiVersion: "2023-09-01"
    name: "[parameters('nsgName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    properties:
      securityRules:
        # ============================================================
        # MANAGEMENT RULES - Restricted to known team IPs/VPN ranges
        # ============================================================

        - name: Allow-SSH-FromTrustedSources
          properties:
            description: >-
              Allow SSH from explicitly allowlisted team IPs only.
              Prefer Azure Bastion or JIT VM Access instead.
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "22"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedManagementSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1000
            direction: Inbound

        - name: Allow-RDP-FromTrustedSources
          properties:
            description: >-
              Allow RDP from explicitly allowlisted team IPs only.
              Prefer Azure Bastion or JIT VM Access instead.
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "3389"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedManagementSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1010
            direction: Inbound

        # ============================================================
        # WEB TRAFFIC RULES - Can be broader if serving public content
        # ============================================================

        - name: Allow-HTTPS-Inbound
          properties:
            description: Allow HTTPS inbound traffic
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "443"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedWebSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1020
            direction: Inbound

        - name: Allow-HTTP-Inbound
          properties:
            description: >-
              Allow HTTP inbound - consider redirecting to HTTPS at app level
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "80"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedWebSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1030
            direction: Inbound

        # ============================================================
        # EXPLICIT DENY-ALL CATCH-ALL (defense in depth)
        # ============================================================

        - name: Deny-All-Other-Inbound
          properties:
            description: Explicit deny-all for any traffic not matching above rules
            protocol: "*"
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "*"
            sourceAddressPrefix: "*"
            destinationAddressPrefix: "*"
            access: Deny
            priority: 4096
            direction: Inbound

outputs:
  nsgResourceId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups', parameters('nsgName'))]"

  securityRecommendations:
    type: object
    value:
      recommendation1: >-
        STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Use Azure Bastion instead of exposing SSH/RDP
        directly. It provides browser-based access without public IP exposure.
      recommendation2: >-
        STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud JIT
        (Just-In-Time) VM Access to open management ports only when needed
        and auto-close them after a time window.
      recommendation3: >-
        For globally distributed teams, deploy a VPN Gateway or use
        Azure VPN Point-to-Site so all team traffic egresses from known IPs.
      recommendation4: >-
        Enable NSG Flow Logs and send to Log Analytics for audit and
        threat detection on all management port access.
